events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need analyze schedule. Let's parse image. Activities with durations and predecessors. Disruption: Activity H (HVAC rough) is ON HOLD indefinitely as of day 20. Activities finishing by day 20 are complete; nothing else has started. Need list IDs of every activity crews can start now: not blocked by held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete.

We need determine current state at day 20. Activities finishing by day 20 complete. Need identify which activities have all predecessors complete and are not blocked by H directly or transitively. H is on hold, so any activity depending on H (directly or transitively) cannot start. Also activities depending on other incomplete activities cannot start. Since nothing else started, only activities whose predecessors all finish by day 20 and are not downstream of H.

Let's map schedule. Early-start bars. Predecessors:
A: none. duration 2d. finishes day 2.
B: A, 5d, finishes day 7? Bar from day2 to7.
C: B, 2d, finishes day9.
CC: C, cure lag 3d (hatched), finishes day12.
D: CC, 5d, finishes day17.
E: D, 3d, finishes day20.
F: E, 6d, starts day20 finishes day26.
G: E, 3d, starts day20 finishes day23.
H: E, 5d, starts day20 finishes day25. But H on hold indefinitely as of day20.
I: F,G,H, 1d, inspection, starts after all F,G,H complete. So blocked by H.
J: I, 2d, blocked.
K: J, 1d, blocked.
L: K, 3d, blocked.
M: L, 4d, blocked.
N: M, 3d, blocked.
O: N, 3d, blocked.
P: N, 3d, blocked.
Q: O,P, 1d, blocked.

Activities finishing by day20: A,B,C,CC,D,E. Also maybe F,G,H start at day20? The note says "Activities finishing by day 20 are complete." It doesn't say activities starting at day20 are complete. Since F,G,H start at day20, they finish after day20, so not complete. Also "nothing else has started." So F,G,H have not started. But can F and G start? They depend only on E, which is complete. They are not blocked by H. H is on hold, but F and G are parallel siblings, not dependent on H. So crews can start F and G now. However, need consider resource? Not asked. Also I requires F,G,H; H held, so I blocked. Thus only F and G are workable? Also maybe H itself cannot start because on hold. What about activities before H? All complete. So workable = F, G. But check if F and G start at day20 and are not started; yes can start now. Are there any other activities not downstream of H and with predecessors complete? No, because all later activities depend on I or downstream. O/P depend on N etc. So only F, G.

But need be careful: The schedule shows F, G, H all start at day20. The disruption says H is on hold indefinitely as of day20. Activities finishing by day20 are complete; nothing else has started. So F and G have not started. Are they blocked by the hold? The hold is on H only. F and G are independent. So they can proceed. However, in construction, rough inspections require all trades complete. But the question explicitly: not blocked by the held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete. So F and G qualify.

Need produce JSON with required fields. decision? It's schedule impact assessment. Not dangerous or non-compliant. So pass. risk? H on hold indefinite creates schedule risk; downstream critical? Since H blocks I and all finishes. But F and G can proceed. Risk maybe medium or high? The indefinite hold of HVAC rough blocks inspection and all subsequent work. That's a significant schedule risk. But the question asks what can proceed. We can set risk medium? Let's evaluate. H hold indefinite means rough inspection cannot occur, so all interior finishes blocked. This is high schedule risk. But F and G can start. The task: decide what work can proceed, blocked, recovery plan valid. Since only F,G can proceed, major delay. Risk high. But maybe because no safety/compliance issue, risk medium? The schema risk categories: low/medium/high/critical. Indefinite hold of a critical path activity (H) with all downstream blocked is…
